title: Rio Tinto to Explore Potential of Pongamia Seeds as Feedstock for Renewable Diesel
content: Global materials and commodities manufacutring giant Rio Tinto has initiated a project to explore the potential of Pongamia seeds as a feedstock for Renewable Diesel. The farms will be set-up in a land area of approximately 3000ha in Queensland, Australia. The effectiveness of the crop will only be assessed 5 years when the crops reach maturity. Pongamia trees, which are native to northern Australia and parts of Asia, possess properties such as high oil and protein content along with carbon capture ability post-seed harvest that make could make them viable sources of biofuel feedstock.
author: Analysts Account
